name: American Elderberry
summary: Distinct from the red elderberry already in this guide — American elderberry thrives in moist edges, roadsides, and productive lower slopes rather than cool rocky hillsides. Large flat-topped white flower clusters in June followed by heavy crops of dark purple-black berries that are critical food for 40+ bird species during migration.
notes: Plant at forest edges, moist margins, and path edges — not deep forest interior. Spreads by root suckers into productive thickets. Flowers attract native bees and butterflies. Do not confuse with red elderberry (Sambucus racemosa) — different niche, different berry timing, different elevation preference. Both belong in this restoration.

planting.soil: Rich, moist; forest edges and roadsides
planting.site_notes: Plant at moist forest edges, path margins, and roadsides — NOT deep forest interior. Different niche from red elderberry. Spreads by root suckers into productive thickets. No deer protection needed. Heavy fruit crop for 40+ migrating bird species.
